title: Anthopterus molaui
taxonomy: Anthopterus molaui is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This family consists of approximately 100 genera and over 2,000 species of ferns, mainly epiphytic and terrestrial. Anthopterus molaui is a specific species of the Anthopterus genus, which includes several other species of ferns found in the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The classification of this plant is based on its morphological characteristics, such as leaf shape and size, as well as genetic analysis. Further research and classification may be necessary to confirm the exact taxonomy of Anthopterus molaui.

Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
